formatted_address: 21106 Kenswick Meadows Ct, Humble, TX 77338, USA
pitch_rationale: The homes in this Humble, TX neighborhood (Houston metro, Gulf Coast climate) display classic multi-hip roof forms with short-to-moderate ridgelines relative to overall footprint, consistent with a 6:12 pitch. Ground shadows cast from the eaves are modest in length — not long enough to suggest steep pitches (8:12+), but clearly present, ruling out very shallow slopes (3:12–4:12). The visible roof plane area from the nadir (overhead) view is moderate, indicating a mid-range slope. Shingle texture is visible with mild shadow relief across the planes. Regional norms for suburban Houston-area construction of this style and era strongly favor 5:12–7:12 hip roofs, with 6:12 being the most prevalent standard pitch, making it the best single estimate given all converging visual cues.
footprint_rationale: Subject home identified as the large multi-section hip-roofed structure at the base of the Kenswick Meadows Ct cul-de-sac (upper-left quadrant), with driveway curving from the cul-de-sac circle. Bounding box traced at approximately x:235–420 px, y:545–875 px (185 px wide × 330 px tall = ~39 ft × ~70 ft). The plan is L-shaped with two main hip-roof sections; an 82% fill factor applied to the gross rectangle gives ~2,250 sq ft. Roof is an all-hip style (no rake/gable ends): two principal ridges plus a connector ridge totaling ~55 ft, approximately eight hip lines at corners totaling ~102 ft, two interior valleys where sections meet totaling ~19 ft, and continuous eave lines around the full perimeter totaling ~187 ft. Confidence is moderate-low due to the oblique angle of some roof sections, partial shadowing, and uncertainty in pinpointing the exact lot boundary of 21106 among similarly styled neighboring homes on the cul-de-sac.
